seoTitle: High Range vs Higher Macdonald — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between High Range and Higher Macdonald. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: High Range vs Higher Macdonald: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in High Range and Higher Macdonald.
heading: High Range vs Higher Macdonald — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between High Range, New South Wales and Higher Macdonald, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: High Range has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Higher Macdonald (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is High Range or Higher Macdonald safer from flooding?
faq.answer: High Range has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Higher Macdonald scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: High Range scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Higher Macdonald scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in High Range or Higher Macdonald?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. High Range has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.27% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
